---
title: Relative rural wage gains outpace nonrural
description: Line chart showing percentage wage changes since the Great Recession
  for rural and nonrural areas.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-wage-change-since-gr-lc/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Labor Market
  - Economic Indicators
tags:
  - QCEW
  - Wages
  - Rural vs. Nonrural
  - Percentage Change
  - Line Chart
image: X_wage_change_since_GR_lc.svg
chart:
  title: Relative rural wage gains outpace nonrural
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  source: Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor
  sourceUrl: https://www.bls.gov/cew/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - qcew-employment-wages
  - bls-cpi-deflators
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This chart shows percentage change in wages since 2007, demonstrating stronger relative wage growth in rural areas.](./X_wage_change_since_GR_lc.svg)

## Overview

This chart shows percentage change in wages since 2007, demonstrating stronger relative wage growth in rural areas.

## Key Findings

- Rural wage growth has outpaced nonrural in percentage terms
- Real wages in both areas have increased 20-30% since 2007

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/wage_change_lc.R` in the producing project.


::: {.callout-note}
## Dangling references

The following slugs are referenced by this project but do not yet have nodes in Dataverse. They are intentionally preserved as future content needs:

- `dataset/bls-cpi-deflators`
:::
